notes: New skill: 15/15 (100%). Old skill: 14/15 (93%). The one old-skill failure is exactly the targeted fix: on maxi-repo it again invented extra-mandate gaps (db unreferenced, workspace protocol note) and grepped source to 'verify' edges (11 tool uses, 68.8s vs new skill's 9 uses, 55.9s).

Gap discipline confirmed across all three evals: new-skill gaps were [] (maxi), [] (mini), exactly-one missing-manifest gap (edge-repo). Old skill emitted 2-3 extra gaps per repo (protocol notes, eslint toolchain, CJS/ESM mismatch — B1/B3 territory).
Source-crawl ban worked: new-skill runs stayed manifest-only (6-9 tool uses); old-skill maxi run grepped source imports again.
Time/tokens: new skill 55.3s vs 58.6s, tokens a wash. The benchmark table's delta column is old-minus-new (config order alphabetical); read it as new skill +7pts pass rate, -3.3s.

Remaining open item (spec-level, not skill text): deriveDispatchOrder weighs dev-only edges like runtime edges; skill now states this honestly. If dev edges should not constrain dispatch order, that's a dispatch-order.mjs change.
